title: Claude 2026 Prompting and Claude Code Practice · Transfer Evaluation
summary: Apply current Anthropic guidance to tool use, examples, context, and long-running coding. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a Claude-specific prompt and eval suite with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use clear direct instructions, motivation, and relevant context for current Claude models.
Use diverse examples and structural tags when they improve task boundary recognition.
Steer tool selection with descriptions and instructions, using enforced tool choice only when required.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.
